title: Berlin Slang - Quick Summary
items: **Berlinerisch is Berlin city speech:** direct, clipped, dry, funny and less formal than standard German.
**The most useful visitor words are Kiez, Späti, Schnauze, Schrippe and JWD:** they explain how Berliners talk about daily life.
**Listen for the sound shifts:** ich becomes ick, das becomes dit or det, keine becomes keene, gut becomes jut and ganz becomes janz.
**Do not perform the dialect too hard:** a few natural words are fine, but forced Berlinerisch quickly sounds like parody.
**The slang reveals the city:** Kiez shows local loyalty, Späti shows everyday Berlin and Schnauze explains the blunt humour.
